lowCode.challenge: Financial institutions operated with decentralized, fragmented legacy systems, creating integration bottlenecks, code duplication, and complex maintenance. Critical business processes (underwriting, pricing, contract management) were scattered across multiple non-integrated systems. The enterprise needed a centralized integration architecture to efficiently orchestrate systems and reduce complexity.
lowCode.solution: Participated in designing and technical leadership of a centralized integration platform (services broker) based on modern middleware architecture. The platform orchestrates connections between legacy and modern systems via Apache Camel, ActiveMQ, and RabbitMQ for distributed messaging. FIX protocol implementation (QuickFIX/J) for direct connection to market data providers. Architecture of 30+ REST APIs supporting critical business domains (underwriting, pricing, contract management). Establishment of secure integration patterns, distributed transaction management, and systems health monitoring. Deployment on AWS with Docker and Kubernetes for container orchestration and automatic scalability.
lowCode.results: The platform now serves 3 major European financial institutions with 99.9% production availability. 40% reduction in time-to-market for new integrations. Processing of high transaction volumes (high-frequency trading decisions, massive insurance flows) without performance degradation. Resolution of 200+ critical integration issues and deployment of 40+ major releases. 35% operational savings through consolidation and automation.
highlights: Participated in design and technical leadership of a services broker platform for critical financial systems
Implemented FIX protocol (QuickFIX/J) for connection to capital markets and market data providers
Architected and delivered 30+ REST APIs for strategic business processes
Middleware orchestration with Apache Camel, ActiveMQ, RabbitMQ for distributed messaging management
High-volume transactional flow management with secure integration patterns
AWS deployment with Docker and Kubernetes for scalability and resilience
Distributed transactions, systems health monitoring, and proactive alerting
Critical production support ensuring 99.9% availability
Mentoring junior developers and establishing integration best practices
Participation in platform evolution to integrate AI/ML capabilities

pijiz.challenge: A leading European Tier-1 insurance company needed to rapidly deliver insurance solutions covering IARD (Damage/P&C) and Health domains, with integration to legacy business systems and support for critical processes (underwriting, pricing, contract management).
pijiz.solution: Designed and delivered a microservices architecture orchestrating critical insurance services. Development of a suite of 30+ REST APIs exposing business processes. Integration with legacy pricing and underwriting systems. Implementation of distributed transaction patterns and business consistency. Migration to AWS and deployment in Kubernetes containers with real-time monitoring and alerting.
pijiz.results: Solutions in production serving hundreds of thousands of customers. 30% reduction in underwriting timelines. Automated processing of 95% of insurance requests. 99.95% production availability. 40% reduction in operational costs through automation. Successful expansion to European markets.
highlights: Microservices architecture for Tier-1 insurance solutions covering IARD and Health
Design and delivery of 30+ REST APIs for critical processes (underwriting, pricing, contract management)
Integration with legacy underwriting and pricing systems
Distributed transaction patterns and business consistency management
AWS deployment in Kubernetes containers with high availability
Real-time monitoring and production incident management
Multi-language and multi-currency support
Compliance with European insurance regulations

banking.challenge: Financial institutions operated with fragmented connections to capital markets, data providers, and clearing services. Legacy systems couldn't handle modern trading volumes, and integration lag impacted competitiveness.
banking.solution: Direct implementation of FIX (Financial Information eXchange) protocol via QuickFIX/J for critical connection to exchanges and market data providers. Real-time integration architecture managing high-frequency trading decisions. Implementation of automatic reconnection mechanisms, lost order handling, and market state reconciliation. Latency optimizations for algorithmic trading. Dedicated monitoring and FIX connection health channel alerts.
banking.results: Robust and high-performance connections to major markets. Support of critical trading volumes without data loss. 60% latency reduction compared to previous solutions. 99.99% reliability on critical FIX channels. Successful expansion of algorithmic trading with new strategies.
highlights: Direct FIX protocol implementation (QuickFIX/J) for connection to exchanges and capital markets
Real-time architecture for high-frequency trading decision support
Automatic reconnection and market state reconciliation
Latency optimizations for algorithmic trading
Robust order and critical transactional flow management
Dedicated monitoring and proactive FIX health alerts
99.99% reliability on critical channels
Multi-broker and multiple exchange support
